{{Infobox concept
| name = Dreadgods
| introduced = cradle-01-unsouled
}}

''See also: [[Dreadgods Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|Dreadgods are massive, highly intelligent, and destructive entities that serve as a fundamental corruption of the natural order on [[Cradle]]. Driven by an inherent hunger, these creatures are capable of consuming entire mountains and devastating landscapes simply through their movement or presence. They are characterized by a hunger-based [[madra]] aspect and possess the ability to travel through space, though they are conceptually heavy. While they can be driven off by the combined efforts of powerful [[Monarchs]], they are considered nearly impossible to kill; attempting to slay one causes the others to awaken and grow stronger, ultimately leading to the death's reintegration. The known Dreadgods include the [[Bleeding Phoenix]], the [[Wandering Titan]], the [[Silent King]], and the [[Weeping Dragon]], with a fifth entity known as the [[Empty Ghost]] also identified. They are often associated with various cults, such as [[Redmoon Hall]] and [[Abyssal Palace]], which follow them to scavenge power or serve their masters. These cultists often utilize [[Blood Shadows]]—entities that resemble [[Remnants]] and seek hosts to consume. Some theories suggest the Dreadgods originated from a specific birthplace or [[labyrinth]], and that they are a direct consequence of the existence of [[Monarchs]] on the world. Ultimately, their presence is viewed as a heavy price for the continued existence of the [[Monarchs]] and the current state of [[Cradle]].}}

== Unsouled ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|The valley may need saving from the return of the [[Dreadgods]].<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c20">''Unsouled'', chapter 20</ref>}}

== Skysworn ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|The Dreadgods are four immensely powerful monsters large enough to blot out the sky. These disasters watch the world with a hunger for destruction and possess the capability to execute an entire clan if they are detected. While they are believed to be responsible for the destruction of [[Sacred Valley]], none of these entities have ever been killed.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|The [[Dreadgods]] watch the world.<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c2">''Skysworn'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|The [[Dreadgods]] are capable of executing a clan if they detect them.<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c2" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Dreadgods]] are disasters described as four monsters big enough to blot out the sky.<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c7">''Skysworn'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Dreadgods]] are hungry for destruction and extremely powerful.<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c7" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|None of the [[Dreadgods]] have ever been killed.<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c7" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Dreadgods]] are believed to be responsible for the destruction of [[Sacred Valley]].<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c18">''Skysworn'', chapter 18</ref>}}

== Ghostwater ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-05-ghostwater|The [[Dreadgods]] are beginning to act strangely.<ref name="cradle-05-ghostwater-c19">''Ghostwater'', chapter 19</ref>}}

== Underlord ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|[[Dreadgods]] are entities mentioned by [[Eithan]].<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c5">''Underlord'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|[[Eithan]] believes [[Dross]] will eventually dwarf [[Dreadgods]].<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c8">''Underlord'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|The rising of all four [[Dreadgods]] together previously resulted in the eradication of the previous generation of [[Monarchs]].<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c13">''Underlord'', chapter 13</ref>}}

== Uncrowned ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|The Dreadgods introduce a profound sense of uncertainty into the existing balance of power. These entities are capable of causing massive destruction, a threat so great that even [[Monarchs]] have perished in failed attempts to slay them. During the [[Dread War]], three of these beings awoke specifically to defend their brother. Currently, [[Monarchs]] maintain control over how the Dreadgods enter the [[Sacred Valley]].}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|The [[Dreadgods]] add uncertainty to the current balance.<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c4">''Uncrowned'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Dreadgods]] cause massive destruction.<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c15">''Uncrowned'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Monarchs]] have died trying to kill the [[Dreadgods]].<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c15" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|Three [[Dreadgods]] woke to defend their brother during the [[Dread War]].<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c15" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Monarchs]] control the entry of [[Dreadgods]] into [[Sacred Valley]].<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c19">''Uncrowned'', chapter 19</ref>}}

== Wintersteel ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|Images of the four Dreadgods are carved into a stone panel within the [[labyrinth]], where [[Eithan]] eventually directs [[Tiberian]]'s attention toward them. These beings are stirring and possess the capability to wake up, driven by a hunger they must satisfy while awake and wandering. Capable of matching a [[Monarch]] for any length of time, the Dreadgods are seeking the [[labyrinth]] and are expected to invade the [[Blackflame Empire]]. Their presence represents a significant danger, as they are entities that could trample [[Blackflame City]].}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The stone panel in the [[labyrinth]] is carved with images of the four [[Dreadgods]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c1">''Wintersteel'', chapter 1</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|[[Eithan]] pointed [[Tiberian]] toward the [[Dreadgods]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c4">''Wintersteel'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Dreadgods]] are expected to invade the [[Blackflame Empire]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c5">''Wintersteel'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|[[Dreadgods]] are entities that could trample [[Blackflame City]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c8">''Wintersteel'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|[[Dreadgods]] are stirring.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c8" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|[[Dreadgods]] are after the [[labyrinth]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c9">''Wintersteel'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|[[Dreadgods]] are capable of matching a [[Monarch]] for any period of time.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c9" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|[[Dreadgods]] satisfy their hunger while awake and wandering.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c9" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Dreadgods]] represent a danger to the [[Blackflame Empire]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c11">''Wintersteel'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|[[Dreadgods]] are beings that can wake up.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c23">''Wintersteel'', chapter 23</ref>}}

== Bloodline ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Dreadgods]] are described as being only slightly less subtle than the heavens collapsing.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c4">''Bloodline'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|One [[Monarch]] claims to have influence over the [[Dreadgods]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c21">''Bloodline'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|There is a method or knowledge regarding how to kill the [[Dreadgods]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c22">''Bloodline'', chapter 22</ref>}}

== Reaper ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The five Dreadgods, which manifest as a [[dragon]] with clouds, a tiger with a halo, a shelled warrior, and a bird, were born within a maze located in the area. While they have been driven off for now, their previous attacks alongside [[Monarchs]] have carved ravines into the territory surrounding [[Refuge]]. Although [[Eithan]] theorizes they are successful versions of [[dreadbeasts]], it is known that [[Monarchs]] must maintain an equilibrium to sustain them and prevent them from growing too strong. Though a method exists to kill them inside the [[labyrinth]] by removing what anchors them to [[life]], no individual [[Monarch]] can defeat one. [[Reigan Shen]] has expressed desires to both bring the Dreadgods under control and call them together to destroy them, even as they grow stronger than ever.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The maze beneath them is the birthplace of the [[Dreadgods]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c2">''Reaper'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Dreadgods]] are anchored to [[life]] by something that, if removed, would make them mortal.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c2" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|There are five [[Dreadgods]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c2" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The [[Dreadgods]] have been driven off for the moment.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c3">''Reaper'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|Attacks from [[Dreadgods]] or [[Monarchs]] have carved ravines into the territory around [[Refuge]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c4">''Reaper'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The [[Dreadgods]] take the location of the [[Titan]] and [[Phoenix]] into account.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c5">''Reaper'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The [[Dreadgods]] appear as a bird, a shelled warrior, a tiger with a halo, and a [[dragon]] accompanied by clouds.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c9">''Reaper'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|There is a way to kill the [[Dreadgods]] inside the [[labyrinth]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c9" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The door in the [[Ancestor's Tomb]] is marked with symbols of the four [[Dreadgods]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c10">''Reaper'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Reigan Shen]] claimed he wanted to call the [[Dreadgods]] together to destroy them.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c10" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The birthplace of the [[Dreadgods]] is located somewhere in the area.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c11">''Reaper'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[The Phoenix]] is made of liquid [[blood madra]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c12">''Reaper'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Eithan]] theorizes that [[Dreadgods]] are the successful versions of [[dreadbeasts]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c13">''Reaper'', chapter 13</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Reigan Shen]] wants to bring the [[Dreadgods]] under control.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c16">''Reaper'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|No [[Monarch]] can defeat a [[Dreadgod]] individually.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c17">''Reaper'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The [[Dreadgods]] are potential future opponents in the [[labyrinth]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c20">''Reaper'', chapter 20</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Dreadgods]] are beings that the [[Monarchs]] sustain.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c22">''Reaper'', chapter 22</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The origin of the [[Dreadgods]] is a topic discussed by [[Reigan Shen]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c23">''Reaper'', chapter 23</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Monarchs]] sustain the [[Dreadgods]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c25">''Reaper'', chapter 25</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Monarchs]] maintain an equilibrium to prevent the [[Dreadgods]] from growing too strong.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c25" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The [[Dreadgods]] are depicted as statues in the [[World of Night]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c26">''Reaper'', chapter 26</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The [[Dreadgods]] are stronger than ever.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c27">''Reaper'', chapter 27</ref>}}

== Dreadgod ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Awakened]] during an apocalyptic battle, the Dreadgods are ancient entities that have existed for thousands of years and are considered nearly impossible to kill. Their presence on [[Cradle]] is a consequence of [[Monarchs]] upsetting a balance, serving as the price paid for those who remain on the world. While [[Northstrider]] once held a plan regarding them, [[Lindon]] now intends to research these looming threats with the goal of killing them. The Dreadgods are currently pursuing [[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]], even as they move toward [[Malice]]'s territory. Killing one [[Dreadgod]] causes the others to grow stronger and converge until the fallen entity is reborn, a cycle that would only end if the [[Monarchs]] ascended.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Dreadgods]] were all awakened on the day of the apocalyptic battle.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c2">''Dreadgod'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Dreadgods]] represent a looming threat to [[Lindon]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c2" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Dreadgods]] are still here.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c3">''Dreadgod'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Lindon]] intends to research the [[Dreadgods]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c4">''Dreadgod'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Northstrider]] had a plan for the [[Dreadgods]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c4" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|There is a relationship between [[Dreadgods]] and [[Monarchs]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c5">''Dreadgod'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Dreadgods]] are entities that can be left behind.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c5" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Beast King]] is in a battle against the [[Dreadgods]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c7">''Dreadgod'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|All four [[Dreadgods]] are awake at once.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c8">''Dreadgod'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Northstrider]] is busy with the [[Dreadgods]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c10">''Dreadgod'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The presence of [[Monarchs]] on [[Cradle]] allows for the existence of the [[Dreadgods]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c11">''Dreadgod'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Lindon]] wants [[Mercy]] to help fight the [[Dreadgods]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c12">''Dreadgod'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Dreadgods]] are the price paid to remain in [[Cradle]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c12" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Dreadgods]] are the consequence of the [[Monarchs]] upsetting a sort of balance.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c12" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Lindon]] contemplates telling [[Mercy]] about the [[Dreadgods]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c12" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Orthos]] wants the [[Dreadgods]] to be dead.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c15">''Dreadgod'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Redmoon]] sought to borrow the power of the [[Dreadgods]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c17">''Dreadgod'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Reigan Shen]] asked if [[Yerin]] felt proud of alienating allies before the [[Dreadgods]] arrive.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c19">''Dreadgod'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Dreadgods]] are after [[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c19" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|If a [[Dreadgod]] is killed, it draws the others and they all become stronger until the slain one is reborn.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c21">''Dreadgod'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Dreadgods]] have lived for thousands of years and are considered all but impossible to kill.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c21" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Dreadgods]] would go away if the [[Monarchs]] ascended.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c22">''Dreadgod'', chapter 22</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Dreadgods]] are coming to [[Malice]]'s territory.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c23">''Dreadgod'', chapter 23</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Lindon]]'s goal is to kill the [[Dreadgods]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c23" />}}

== Waybound ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The Dreadgods are intelligent, clever beings fundamentally designed to devour the world, though they are not complete monsters like the [[Monarchs]]. Because of their immense weight, they must utilize tricks such as trading with objects of equal mass to move, yet when empowered, they can travel faster than ever. While [[Malice]] allowed them to exist and they loom large in various futures, the two remaining Dreadgods eventually began a rampage that destroyed [[Reigan Shen]]'s continent. [[Lindon]] sought to finish them and remove them from [[Cradle]], ultimately losing consciousness during the process of killing them. Following their defeat, it is suggested that the Dreadgods might be sealed within a [[labyrinth]].}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Lindon]] intends to prepare [[Orthos]] and [[Little Blue]] to fight the [[Dreadgods]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c3">''Waybound'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Dreadgods]] are intelligent.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c4">''Waybound'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Dreadgods]] are not complete monsters like the [[Monarchs]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c7">''Waybound'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Yerin]] and [[Lindon]] are looking to get the [[Dreadgods]] out of [[Cradle]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c8">''Waybound'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Yerin]] is planning on facing down both [[Dreadgods]] and [[Monarchs]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c8" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The empowered [[Dreadgods]] can move faster than ever.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c9">''Waybound'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Lindon]] questions letting the [[Dreadgods]] live for centuries.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c10">''Waybound'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Dreadgods]] killed the [[Monarch]] who invented a certain technique.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c12">''Waybound'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Dreadgods]] are clashing in the sky.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c15">''Waybound'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Dreadgods]] are too heavy to move and must use tricks like trading with something of equal weight to transport themselves.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c21">''Waybound'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|There are two [[Dreadgods]] left.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c23">''Waybound'', chapter 23</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Dreadgods]] are currently rampaging.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c23" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|By killing the [[Dreadgods]], [[Lindon]] put himself on a timer.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c23" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Dreadgods]] are clever and have always possessed intelligence.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c24">''Waybound'', chapter 24</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Dreadgods]] are fundamentally made to devour the world.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The intelligent population of the world could keep the [[Dreadgods]] fed with minimal loss of [[life]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c25">''Waybound'', chapter 25</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Dreadgods]] loom large in the futures [[Malice]] glimpses.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c26">''Waybound'', chapter 26</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Monarchs]] can go toe-to-toe with [[Dreadgods]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c27">''Waybound'', chapter 27</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Malice]] allowed the [[Dreadgods]] to exist.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c28">''Waybound'', chapter 28</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Dreadgods]] are closing in on [[Lindon]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c29">''Waybound'', chapter 29</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Dreadgods]] are destroying [[Reigan Shen]]'s continent.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c30">''Waybound'', chapter 30</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Lindon]]'s goal is to finish the [[Dreadgods]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c30" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Lindon]] lost consciousness while killing the [[Dreadgods]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c33">''Waybound'', chapter 33</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Dreadgods]] might be sealed in a [[labyrinth]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c35">''Waybound'', chapter 35</ref>}}

== Threshold: Stories from Cradle ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|The [[Dreadgods]] caused untold devastation to the [[Blackflame Empire]].<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c1">''Threshold: Stories from Cradle'', chapter 1</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Dreadgods]] possess immense power.<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c3">''Threshold: Stories from Cradle'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|Some people come to the fighting [[arena]] to escape the [[Dreadgods]].<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c8">''Threshold: Stories from Cradle'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|The enemy in the [[Iteration]] reminds [[Pride]] of the [[Dreadgods]].<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c10">''Threshold: Stories from Cradle'', chapter 10</ref>}}

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|“I would like to ensure that the Blackflame Empire is evacuated before the danger of the Dreadgods.”<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c11" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|“The maze beneath us is the birthplace of the Dreadgods,” Elder Whisper went on,<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c2" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|“There’s a way to kill the Dreadgods in there?” Ziel asked.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c9" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|“Disturbing implications, but that was always one of the strongest theories about the origins of the dreadbeasts. That they were the failures, and the successes were the Dreadgods.”<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c13" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|“I presume you understand the origin of the Dreadgods better now, unless Eithan already found a way to tell you.”<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c23" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Cradle nav}}

[[Category:Concepts]]
[[Category:Unsouled]]
[[Category:Skysworn]]
[[Category:Ghostwater]]
[[Category:Underlord]]
[[Category:Uncrowned]]
[[Category:Wintersteel]]
[[Category:Bloodline]]
[[Category:Reaper]]
[[Category:Dreadgod]]
[[Category:Waybound]]
[[Category:Threshold: Stories from Cradle]]
[[Category:Cradle]]
[[Category:Cradle concepts]]
[[Category:Lifeforms]]
